prompt: Say: I have two brothers and one sister.
correctAnswer: Ho due fratelli e una sorella.
hints: Practice this phrase out loud.

prompt: Ask: Do you have any children?
correctAnswer: Hai dei figli?
hints: Practice this phrase out loud.

prompt: Say: My parents live in the countryside.
correctAnswer: I miei genitori vivono in campagna.
hints: Practice this phrase out loud.

prompt: Say: My grandmother is seventy years old.
correctAnswer: Mia nonna ha settanta anni.
hints: State age with 'is ... years old'.

prompt: Ask: Is she your sister?
correctAnswer: E' tua sorella?
hints: Simple yes/no question.

prompt: Say: I live with my family.
correctAnswer: Vivo con la mia famiglia.
hints: A short, common phrase.

prompt: Vocabulary: your mother's mother
correctAnswer: nonna
hints: Think of the female relative two generations above you.

prompt: Say: My best friend's name is Sam.
correctAnswer: Il mio migliore amico si chiama Sam.
hints: Use the possessive apostrophe: friend's.

prompt: Ask: How old is your brother?
correctAnswer: Quanti anni ha tuo fratello?
hints: Use 'how old is' to ask about age.

prompt: Say: I have a dog and a cat at home.
correctAnswer: Ho un cane e un gatto a casa.
hints: List two things joined with 'and'.

prompt: Say: I visit my grandparents on Sundays.
correctAnswer: Vado a trovare i miei nonni la domenica.
hints: Family routine phrase.

prompt: Ask: Do you have any brothers or sisters?
correctAnswer: Hai fratelli o sorelle?
hints: Basic family question.

prompt: Say: My friend is very kind.
correctAnswer: Il mio amico e' molto gentile.
hints: Simple description.

prompt: Say: My cousin is getting married next spring.
correctAnswer: Mio cugino si sposa la prossima primavera.
hints: Present continuous for a planned future event.

prompt: Say: We usually meet up at the weekends.
correctAnswer: Di solito ci incontriamo nei fine settimana.
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: Ask: How long have you known each other?
correctAnswer: Da quanto vi conoscete?
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: Say: My parents got divorced when I was ten.
correctAnswer: I miei genitori hanno divorziato quando avevo dieci anni.
hints: 'Got divorced' is the past tense phrase.

prompt: Ask: Are you close to your siblings?
correctAnswer: Sei vicino ai tuoi fratelli?
hints: 'Close' here means emotionally connected; siblings are brothers or sisters.

prompt: Say: We are a big family — there are seven of us.
correctAnswer: Siamo una grande famiglia — ci sono sette di noi.
hints: Use 'there are' to state how many people.

prompt: Say: My friend moved abroad for work last year.
correctAnswer: Il mio amico si e' trasferito all'estero per lavoro lo scorso anno.
hints: 'Moved abroad' means relocated to another country.

prompt: Ask: Do you get on well with your neighbors?
correctAnswer: Vai bene con i tuoi vicini?
hints: 'Get on well with' means have a good relationship with.

prompt: Say: We celebrated my grandfather's eightieth birthday last weekend.
correctAnswer: Abbiamo festeggiato il compleanno di mio nonno lo scorso fine settimana.
hints: Use simple past for a completed past event.

prompt: Say: She is my oldest friend — we have known each other since school.
correctAnswer: Lei è la mia più vecchia amica — ci conosciamo da scuola.
hints: 'Oldest friend' means the longest friendship, not oldest in age.

prompt: Say: We celebrated my sister's birthday yesterday.
correctAnswer: Abbiamo festeggiato il compleanno di mia sorella ieri.
hints: Past event sentence.

prompt: Ask: How did you meet your best friend?
correctAnswer: Come hai conosciuto il tuo migliore amico?
hints: Conversation starter.

prompt: Say: I call my parents every evening.
correctAnswer: Chiamo i miei genitori ogni sera.
hints: Routine communication phrase.

prompt: Say: My sister and I do not always see eye to eye, but we support each other.
correctAnswer: Io e mia sorella non vediamo sempre gli occhi, ma ci sosteniamo a vicenda.
hints: 'See eye to eye' means agree.

prompt: Say: We have a family tradition of spending Christmas together every year.
correctAnswer: Abbiamo una tradizione familiare di trascorrere il Natale insieme ogni anno.
hints: Notice the tense or modal used.

prompt: Ask: What was it like growing up in a large family?
correctAnswer: Com'era crescere in una grande famiglia?
hints: 'What was it like' asks about the experience.

prompt: Say: My relationship with my parents has improved since I moved out.
correctAnswer: Il mio rapporto con i miei genitori è migliorato da quando mi sono trasferito.
hints: Present perfect with 'since' for a change that started at a past point.

prompt: Say: I try to keep in touch with old friends, even when life gets busy.
correctAnswer: Cerco di tenere in contatto con vecchi amici, anche quando la vita si fa occupata.
hints: 'Keep in touch' means maintain contact.

prompt: Say: My brother and I have very different personalities, but we have the same sense of humour.
correctAnswer: Io e mio fratello abbiamo personalità molto diverse, ma abbiamo lo stesso senso dell'umorismo.
hints: Contrast with 'but'; 'sense of humour' is the ability to find things funny.

prompt: Choose the best response. A friend asks why you do not visit your family more often.
correctAnswer: Viviamo abbastanza lontano, e io lotta per trovare il tempo intorno impegni di lavoro.
hints: Give a reason that combines distance and schedule.

prompt: Say: Having grown up in a small town, I appreciate the close-knit community there.
correctAnswer: Essendo cresciuto in una piccola città, apprezzo la comunità vicina.
hints: 'Close-knit' means a tight, supportive community.

prompt: Ask: How do you balance time between your family and social life?
correctAnswer: Come si bilancia il tempo tra la famiglia e la vita sociale?
hints: 'Balance' here means manage both fairly.

prompt: Say: We grew apart after university, but we have recently reconnected.
correctAnswer: Siamo cresciuti dopo l'università, ma abbiamo recentemente ricollegato.
hints: 'Grew apart' means drifted away from each other over time.

prompt: Say: Good friendships require trust and honest communication.
correctAnswer: Buone amicizie richiedono fiducia e comunicazione onesta.
hints: B1 relationship statement.

prompt: Ask: What do you do when a friend needs support?
correctAnswer: Cosa fai quando un amico ha bisogno di supporto?
hints: Support-oriented question.

prompt: Say: Family traditions help us stay connected.
correctAnswer: Le tradizioni familiari ci aiutano a rimanere connessi.
hints: Community/family link.

prompt: Say: The dynamics within a blended family can be complex but deeply rewarding.
correctAnswer: Le dinamiche all'interno di una famiglia mescolata possono essere complesse ma profondamente gratificanti.
hints: 'Blended family' = step-parents or half-siblings; 'dynamics' = the way relationships work.

prompt: Say: I was raised to value loyalty and honesty above all else.
correctAnswer: Sono stato elevato a valorizzare la lealtà e l'onestà soprattutto.
hints: Passive 'was raised' refers to how your parents brought you up.

prompt: Say: Intergenerational relationships within a family can reflect broader societal changes.
correctAnswer: Le relazioni intergenerazionali all'interno di una famiglia possono riflettere cambiamenti sociali più ampi.
hints: 'Intergenerational' refers to connections across different generations.

prompt: Say: The expectation that adult children will look after ageing parents varies considerably across cultures.
correctAnswer: L'aspettativa che i bambini adulti si occuperanno dopo l'invecchiamento dei genitori varia notevolmente attraverso le culture.
hints: 'Varies considerably' means differs a lot; 'across cultures' means between different societies.
culturalNote: In many Asian and southern European cultures, multi-generational households are the norm, while nuclear families are more common in northern Europe and North America.

prompt: Say: Maintaining long-distance friendships requires consistent effort and mutual commitment.
correctAnswer: Mantenere amicizie a lunga distanza richiede uno sforzo costante e un impegno reciproco.
hints: 'Mutual commitment' means both people must put in effort.

prompt: Say: The influence of one's upbringing on adult behavior is well-documented in psychology.
correctAnswer: L'influenza della propria educazione sul comportamento degli adulti è ben documentata in psicologia.
hints: 'One's upbringing' = how you were raised; 'well-documented' = supported by evidence.

prompt: Ask: To what extent do you think family shapes individual identity?
correctAnswer: In che misura pensi che la famiglia formi l'identità individuale?
hints: 'To what extent' asks how much or how strongly.

prompt: Say: Chosen family — the network of friends who become like family — is increasingly valued in modern life.
correctAnswer: La famiglia scelta — la rete di amici che diventano come la famiglia — è sempre più apprezzata nella vita moderna.
hints: 'Chosen family' refers to close friends who fill a family-like role.

prompt: Say: Healthy boundaries can strengthen long-term relationships.
correctAnswer: I confini sani possono rafforzare le relazioni a lungo termine.
hints: Advanced relationship concept.

prompt: Ask: How do family expectations influence personal choices?
correctAnswer: Come influiscono le aspettative familiari sulle scelte personali?
hints: Reflective high-level question.

prompt: Say: Friendships evolve as people's priorities change.
correctAnswer: Le amicizie si evolvono mentre le priorità delle persone cambiano.
hints: Nuanced change-over-time sentence.
